REPLY IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING ACCES FOR INSPECTION OF DWELLINGS LOCATED AT 324-330 CONNECTICUT STREET IN SAN FRANCISCO FILED BY PLAINTIFF MCGOWAN, PATRICK MCDERMOTT, PAUL

DISCOVERY 610, PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING ACCESS FOR INSPECTION OF DWELLINGS LOCATED AT 324-330 CONNECTICUT STREET IN SAN FRANCISCO GRANTED, AS FOLLOWS: 1. PLAINTIFFS, THEIR COUNSEL, AND EXPERTS SHALL HAVE ACCESS TO THE SUBJECT PROPERTY, EXTERIOR AND INTERIOR, FOR A PERIOD NOT TO EXCEED FOUR HOURS; 2. PLAINTIFFS, THEIR COUNSEL, AND THEIR EXPERTS MAY INSPECT THE PROPERTY, BUT MAY NOT CONDUCT ANY DESTRUCTIVE OR INVASIVE TESTING; 3. INSPECTION SHALL BE PERFORMED NOT LESS THAN FIVE DAYS, NOR MORE THAN TEN DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER; 4. IMMEDIATELY UPON RECEIPT OF THIS ORDER, DEFENDANT SHALL PROVIDE WRITTEN NOTICE TO HER TENANTS NOTIFYING THE TENANTS THAT ENTRY INTO THEIR RESPECTIVE UNITS WILL BE MADE AS SET FORTH IN THIS ORDER, AND THAT FURTHER NOTICE OF THE SPECIFIC DATE AND TIME OF ENTRY WILL BE GIVEN 24 HOURS IN ADVANCE BY PERSONAL OR TELEPHONE NOTICE. 5. THE PARTIES SHALL MEET AND CONFER TO DETERMINE A DATE AND TIME WHEN INSPECTION SHALL PROCEED, WITHIN THE TIME PERIOD SPECIFIED IN THIS ORDER. ONCE A DEFINITE DATE AND TIME HAS BEEN SET, DEFENDANT OR HER COUNSEL SHALL PROVIDE EITHER PERSONAL OR TELEPHONIC NOTICE TO THE AFFECTED TENANTS, AT LEAST 24 HOURS IN ADVANCE. 6. THE PARTIES SHALL COOPERATE IN ACCESSING THE SUBJECT PREMISES FOR INSPECTION. IF EITHER PARTY WISHES TO PRESENT ARGUMENT ON THIS MOTION, THE MATTER WILL BE CONTINUED TO MONDAY, MARCH 23, 2009 AT 10:30 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 610 FOR HEARING. COMMISSIONER: PAUL SLAVIT. NOT REPORTED.

Notice Of Motion For Order Granting Access For Inspection Of Dwellings Located At 324-330 Connecticut Street In San Francisco
Off Calendar Mar-23-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:

Appearance:
Betty S. Hsu-Nordstedt, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Patrick McGowan
No other appearance, tentative ruling is adopted as modified. Plaintiffs' Motion For Order Granting Access For Inspection Of Dwellings Located At 324-330 Connecticut Street In San Francisco is granted, as follows:
1. Plaintiffs, their counsel, and experts shall have access to the subject property, exterior and interior, for a period not to exceed four hours;
2. Plaintiffs, their counsel, and their experts may inspect the property, but may not conduct any destructive or invasive testing;
3. Inspection shall be performed not less than two days, nor more than ten days from the date of this order;
4. Immediately upon receipt of this order, defendant shall provide written notice to her tenants notifying the tenants that entry into their respective units will be made as set forth in this order, and that further notice of the specific date and time of entry will be given 24 hours in advance by personal or telephone notice.
5. The parties shall meet and confer to determine a date and time when inspection shall proceed, within the time period specified in this order. Once a definite date and time has been set, defendant or her counsel shall provide either personal or telephonic notice to the affected tenants, at least 24 hours in advance.
6. The parties shall cooperate in accessing the subject premises for inspection. Order signed in open court. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion For Order Granting Access For Inspection Of Dwellings Located At 324-330 Connecticut Street In San Francisco
Off Calendar Mar-20-2009 Continued To Mar-23-09 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
MINI MINUTES: Plaintiffs' Motion for order granting access for inspection of dwellings located at 324-330 Connecticut Street in San Francisco granted, as follows:
1. Plaintiffs, their counsel, and experts shall have access to the subject property, exterior and interior, for a period not to exceed four hours;
2. Plaintiffs, their counsel, and their experts may inspect the property, but may not conduct any destructive or invasive testing;
3. Inspection shall be performed not less than five days, nor more than ten days from the date of this order;
4. Immediately upon receipt of this order, defendant shall provide written notice to her tenants notifying the tenants that entry into their respective units will be made as set forth in this order, and that further notice of the specific date and time of entry will be given 24 hours in advance by personal or telephone notice.
5. The parties shall meet and confer to determine a date and time when inspection shall proceed, within the time period specified in this order. Once a definite date and time has been set, defendant or her counsel shall provide either personal or telephonic notice to the affected tenants, at least 24 hours in advance.
6. The parties shall cooperate in accessing the subject premises for inspection.
If either party wishes to present argument on this motion, the matter will be continued to Monday, March 23, 2009 at 10:30 a.m. in Department 610 for hearing. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported. (610/PS)
